channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:

item.title: 3I/ATLAS is only the third confirmed interstellar object ever detected passing through our solar system. It may be older than the Sun itself, and Webb observations show it is carrying an unusually carbon dioxide-rich coma as it swings past once before leaving fo - Space Daily
item.description:
1. 3I/ATLAS is only the third confirmed interstellar object ever detected passing through our solar system. It may be older than the Sun itself, and Webb observations show it is carrying an unusually carbon dioxide-rich coma as it swings past once before leaving fo  Space Daily
  2. NASA’s Webb Detects Methane on Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. SETI Institute Looks for Signs of Technology in Interstellar Visitor 3I/ATLAS  SETI
  4. Scientists probe cosmic visitor from deep space, come up empty in search for alien life  Los Angeles Times
  5. The SETI Institute Releases Technosignature Report on 3I/ATLAS  Universe Today

item.title: A South Dakota rancher followed his gut, found dinosaur teeth on his land, and unwittingly led experts to a 38-foot T. rex skeleton now heading to auction where a billionaire may happily pay $30 million+ leading to millions in windfall for his widow - Luxurylaunches
item.description:
1. A South Dakota rancher followed his gut, found dinosaur teeth on his land, and unwittingly led experts to a 38-foot T. rex skeleton now heading to auction where a billionaire may happily pay $30 million+ leading to millions in windfall for his widow  Luxurylaunches
  2. T. rex fossil found on South Dakota ranch could sell for $30 million  Yahoo
  3. A Tyrannosaurus Skeleton to Be Auctioned at Sotheby’s  Inbox.eu
  4. Massive T. rex skeleton named Gus heads to auction with record estimate  Audacy
  5. Jurassic giant: World’s most complete T-Rex fossil could sell for $30 million at auction  FOX Weather

item.title: The James Webb telescope keeps finding early galaxies that look brighter, bigger and more mature than astronomers expected, forcing researchers to rethink how quickly the first galaxies formed stars and assembled after the Big Bang. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The James Webb telescope keeps finding early galaxies that look brighter, bigger and more mature than astronomers expected, forcing researchers to rethink how quickly the first galaxies formed stars and assembled after the Big Bang.  Space Daily
  2. Why Webb Is Rewriting The Story Of The Early Universe  WorldAtlas
  3. The Galaxy That Forgot to Spin  Universe Today
  4. 'Astonishing': James Webb telescope spots the most chemically primitive galaxy in the ancient universe  Live Science

item.title: Scientists Simulated Nuclear Fallout in The Lab And Got a Surprise - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Scientists Simulated Nuclear Fallout in The Lab And Got a Surprise  ScienceAlert
  2. Scientists Recreate a Nuclear Fireball and Uncover Fallout’s Hidden Chemistry  SciTechDaily
  3. What happens seconds after a nuclear explosion continues to surprise experts: the key lies in radioactive fallout  Revista Merca2.0
  4. Scientists simulate nuclear fireball in the laboratory and discover a surprise in the radioactive fallout by observing how cesium, uranium, and cerium change when they remain at high temperatures for a longer time.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  5. Science breakthrough: Scientists create a miniature nuclear fireball – and make a surprise discovery  GB News
